class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.seed
documentation: Seed to control random procedure. If nothing is given, the API generate the seed randomly, use it for sampling, and return the seed along with the generated result. When using the `n` argument, you can pass a list of seed values to control all of the independent generations.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.stop
documentation:
When one of the stop phrases appears in the generation result, the API will stop generation.
The stop phrases are excluded from the result.
This option is incompatible with beam search (specified by `num_beams`); use `stop_tokens` for that case instead.
Defaults to empty list.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.stop_tokens
documentation:
Stop generating further tokens when generated token corresponds to any of the tokens in the sequence.
If beam search is enabled, all of the active beams should contain the stop token to terminate generation.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.stream
documentation: Whether to stream generation result. When set true, each token will be sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated. Not supported when using beam search.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.temperature
documentation: Sampling temperature. Smaller temperature makes the generation result closer to greedy, argmax (i.e., `top_k = 1`) sampling. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`temperature`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.temperature) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.timeout_microseconds
documentation: Request timeout. Gives the `HTTP 429 Too Many Requests` response status code. Default behavior is no timeout.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.token_index_to_replace
documentation: A list of token indices where to replace the embeddings of input tokens provided via either `tokens` or `prompt`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.top_k
documentation: The number of highest probability tokens to keep for sampling. Numbers between 0 and the vocab size of the model (both inclusive) are allowed. The default value is 0, which means that the API does not apply top-k filtering. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`top_k`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_k) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.top_p
documentation: Tokens comprising the top `top_p` probability mass are kept for sampling. Numbers between 0.0 (exclusive) and 1.0 (inclusive) are allowed. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`top_p`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_p) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.prompt
documentation: The prompt (i.e., input text) to generate completions for. Either `prompt` or `tokens` field is required.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.model
documentation: ID of target endpoint. If you want to send request to specific adapter, using \"ENDPOINT_ID:ADAPTER_ROUTE\" format.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.bad_word_tokens
documentation: Same as the above `bad_words` field, but receives token sequences instead of text phrases. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`bad_word_ids`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.bad_words_ids) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.bad_words
documentation:
Text phrases that should not be generated.
For a bad word phrase that contains N tokens, if the first N-1 tokens appears at the last of the generated result, the logit for the last token of the phrase is set to -inf.
Before checking whether a bard word is included in the result, the word is converted into tokens.
We recommend using `bad_word_tokens` because it is clearer.
For example, after tokenization, phrases \"clear\" and \" clear\" can result in different token sequences due to the prepended space character.
Defaults to empty list.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.beam_search_type
documentation: One of `DETERMINISTIC`, `NAIVE_SAMPLING`, and `STOCHASTIC`. Which beam search type to use. `DETERMINISTIC` means the standard, deterministic beam search, which is similar to Hugging Face's [`beam_search`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationMixin.beam_search). Argmuents for controlling random sampling such as `top_k` and `top_p` are not allowed for this option. `NAIVE_SAMPLING` is similar to Hugging Face's [`beam_sample`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationMixin.beam_sample). `STOCHASTIC` means stochastic beam search (more details in [Kool et al. (2019)](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v97/kool19a.html)). This option is ignored if `num_beams` is not provided. Defaults to `DETERMINISTIC`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.early_stopping
documentation: Whether to stop the beam search when at least `num_beams` beams are finished with the EOS token. Only allowed for beam search. Defaults to false. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`early_stopping`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.early_stopping) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.embedding_to_replace
documentation: A list of flattened embedding vectors used for replacing the tokens at the specified indices provided via `token_index_to_replace`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.encoder_no_repeat_ngram
documentation: If this exceeds 1, every ngram of that size occurring in the input token sequence cannot appear in the generated result. 1 means that this mechanism is disabled (i.e., you cannot prevent 1-gram from being generated repeatedly). Only allowed for encoder-decoder models. Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`encoder_no_repeat_ngram_size`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.encoder_no_repeat_ngram_size) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.encoder_repetition_penalty
documentation: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the input tokens. Should be greater than or equal to 1.0. 1.0 means no penalty. Only allowed for encoder-decoder models. See [Keskar et al., 2019](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.05858) for more details. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`encoder_repetition_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.encoder_repetition_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.eos_token
documentation: A list of endpoint sentence tokens.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.forced_output_tokens
documentation: A token sequence that is enforced as a generation output. This option can be used when evaluating the model for the datasets with multi-choice problems (e.g., [HellaSwag](https://huggingface.co/datasets/hellaswag), [MMLU](https://huggingface.co/datasets/cais/mmlu)). Use this option with `include_output_logprobs` to get logprobs for the evaluation.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.frequency_penalty
documentation: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled, taking into account their frequency in the preceding text. This penalization diminishes the model's tendency to reproduce identical lines verbatim.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.include_output_logits
documentation: Whether to include the output logits to the generation output.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.include_output_logprobs
documentation: Whether to include the output logprobs to the generation output.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.length_penalty
documentation: Coefficient for exponential length penalty that is used with beam search. Only allowed for beam search. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`length_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.length_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.max_tokens
documentation: The maximum number of tokens to generate. For decoder-only models like GPT, the length of your input tokens plus `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum length (e.g., 2048 for OpenAI GPT-3). For encoder-decoder models like T5 or BlenderBot, `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum output length. class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.max_total_tokens

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.min_total_tokens
documentation: The minimum number of tokens including both the generated result and the input tokens. Only allowed for decoder-only models. class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.n

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.num_beams

documentation: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled at least once in the existing text.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionscompletebody.DedicatedCompletionsCompleteBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.repetition_penalty
documentation: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the generated result (plus the input tokens for decoder-only models). Should be greater than or equal to 1.0 (1.0 means no penalty). Only one argument between `max_tokens` and `max_total_tokens` is allowed. Default value is the model's maximum length. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`max_length`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_length) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.min_tokens
documentation: The minimum number of tokens to generate. Default value is 0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`min_new_tokens`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.min_new_tokens) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.min_total_tokens
documentation: The minimum number of tokens including both the generated result and the input tokens. Only allowed for decoder-only models. Only one argument between `min_tokens` and `min_total_tokens` is allowed. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`min_length`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.min_length) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.n
documentation: The number of independently generated results for the prompt. Not supported when using beam search. Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`num_return_sequences`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.num_return_sequences) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.no_repeat_ngram
documentation: If this exceeds 1, every ngram of that size can only occur once among the generated result (plus the input tokens for decoder-only models). 1 means that this mechanism is disabled (i.e., you cannot prevent 1-gram from being generated repeatedly). Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`no_repeat_ngram_size`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.no_repeat_ngram_size) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.num_beams
documentation: Number of beams for beam search. Numbers between 1 and 31 (both inclusive) are allowed. Default behavior is no beam search. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`num_beams`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.num_beams) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.presence_penalty
documentation: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled at least once in the existing text.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.repetition_penalty
documentation: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the generated result (plus the input tokens for decoder-only models). Should be greater than or equal to 1.0 (1.0 means no penalty). See [keskar et al., 2019](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.05858) for more details. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`repetition_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.repetition_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.response_format
documentation:
The enforced format of the model's output.

Note that the content of the output message may be truncated if it exceeds the `max_tokens`.
You can check this by verifying that the `finish_reason` of the output message is `length`.

***Important***
You must explicitly instruct the model to produce the desired output format using a system prompt or user message (e.g., `You are an API generating a valid JSON as output.`).
Otherwise, the model may result in an unending stream of whitespace or other characters.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.seed
documentation: Seed to control random procedure. If nothing is given, the API generate the seed randomly, use it for sampling, and return the seed along with the generated result. When using the `n` argument, you can pass a list of seed values to control all of the independent generations.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.stop
documentation:
When one of the stop phrases appears in the generation result, the API will stop generation.
The stop phrases are excluded from the result.
This option is incompatible with beam search (specified by `num_beams`); use `stop_tokens` for that case instead.
Defaults to empty list.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.stop_tokens
documentation:
Stop generating further tokens when generated token corresponds to any of the tokens in the sequence.
If beam search is enabled, all of the active beams should contain the stop token to terminate generation.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.stream
documentation: Whether to stream generation result. When set true, each token will be sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.temperature
documentation: Sampling temperature. Smaller temperature makes the generation result closer to greedy, argmax (i.e., `top_k = 1`) sampling. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`temperature`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.temperature) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.timeout_microseconds
documentation: Request timeout. Gives the `HTTP 429 Too Many Requests` response status code. Default behavior is no timeout.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.token_index_to_replace
documentation: A list of token indices where to replace the embeddings of input tokens provided via either `tokens` or `prompt`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.top_k
documentation: The number of highest probability tokens to keep for sampling. Numbers between 0 and the vocab size of the model (both inclusive) are allowed. The default value is 0, which means that the API does not apply top-k filtering. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`top_k`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_k) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokensTypedDict.top_p
documentation: Tokens comprising the top `top_p` probability mass are kept for sampling. Numbers between 0.0 (exclusive) and 1.0 (inclusive) are allowed. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`top_p`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_p) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.tokens
documentation: The tokenized prompt (i.e., input tokens). Either `prompt` or `tokens` field is required.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.model
documentation: ID of target endpoint. If you want to send request to specific adapter, using \"ENDPOINT_ID:ADAPTER_ROUTE\" format.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.bad_word_tokens
documentation: Same as the above `bad_words` field, but receives token sequences instead of text phrases. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`bad_word_ids`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.bad_words_ids) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.bad_words
documentation:
Text phrases that should not be generated.
For a bad word phrase that contains N tokens, if the first N-1 tokens appears at the last of the generated result, the logit for the last token of the phrase is set to -inf.
Before checking whether a bard word is included in the result, the word is converted into tokens.
We recommend using `bad_word_tokens` because it is clearer.
For example, after tokenization, phrases \"clear\" and \" clear\" can result in different token sequences due to the prepended space character.
Defaults to empty list.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.beam_search_type
documentation: One of `DETERMINISTIC`, `NAIVE_SAMPLING`, and `STOCHASTIC`. Which beam search type to use. `DETERMINISTIC` means the standard, deterministic beam search, which is similar to Hugging Face's [`beam_search`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationMixin.beam_search). Argmuents for controlling random sampling such as `top_k` and `top_p` are not allowed for this option. `NAIVE_SAMPLING` is similar to Hugging Face's [`beam_sample`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationMixin.beam_sample). `STOCHASTIC` means stochastic beam search (more details in [Kool et al. (2019)](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v97/kool19a.html)). This option is ignored if `num_beams` is not provided. Defaults to `DETERMINISTIC`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.early_stopping
documentation: Whether to stop the beam search when at least `num_beams` beams are finished with the EOS token. Only allowed for beam search. Defaults to false. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`early_stopping`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.early_stopping) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.embedding_to_replace
documentation: A list of flattened embedding vectors used for replacing the tokens at the specified indices provided via `token_index_to_replace`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.encoder_no_repeat_ngram
documentation: If this exceeds 1, every ngram of that size occurring in the input token sequence cannot appear in the generated result. 1 means that this mechanism is disabled (i.e., you cannot prevent 1-gram from being generated repeatedly). Only allowed for encoder-decoder models. Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`encoder_no_repeat_ngram_size`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.encoder_no_repeat_ngram_size) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.encoder_repetition_penalty
documentation: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the input tokens. Should be greater than or equal to 1.0. 1.0 means no penalty. Only allowed for encoder-decoder models. See [Keskar et al., 2019](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.05858) for more details. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`encoder_repetition_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.encoder_repetition_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.eos_token
documentation: A list of endpoint sentence tokens.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.forced_output_tokens
documentation: A token sequence that is enforced as a generation output. This option can be used when evaluating the model for the datasets with multi-choice problems (e.g., [HellaSwag](https://huggingface.co/datasets/hellaswag), [MMLU](https://huggingface.co/datasets/cais/mmlu)). Use this option with `include_output_logprobs` to get logprobs for the evaluation.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.frequency_penalty
documentation: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled, taking into account their frequency in the preceding text. This penalization diminishes the model's tendency to reproduce identical lines verbatim.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.include_output_logits
documentation: Whether to include the output logits to the generation output.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.include_output_logprobs
documentation: Whether to include the output logprobs to the generation output.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.length_penalty
documentation: Coefficient for exponential length penalty that is used with beam search. Only allowed for beam search. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`length_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.length_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithTokens.max_tokens
documentation: The maximum number of tokens to generate. For decoder-only models like GPT, the length of your input tokens plus `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum length (e.g., 2048 for OpenAI GPT-3). For encoder-decoder models like T5 or BlenderBot, `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum output length. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`max_new_tokens`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_new_tokens) argument.

This is similar to Hugging Face's [`max_length`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_length) argument.

documentation: The minimum number of tokens including both the generated result and the input tokens. Only allowed for decoder-only models. Use this option with `include_output_logprobs` to get logprobs for the evaluation.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.frequency_penalty
documentation: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled, taking into account their frequency in the preceding text. This penalization diminishes the model's tendency to reproduce identical lines verbatim.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.include_output_logits
documentation: Whether to include the output logits to the generation output.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.include_output_logprobs
documentation: Whether to include the output logprobs to the generation output.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.length_penalty
documentation: Coefficient for exponential length penalty that is used with beam search. Only allowed for beam search. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`length_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.length_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.max_tokens
documentation: The maximum number of tokens to generate. For decoder-only models like GPT, the length of your input tokens plus `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum length (e.g., 2048 for OpenAI GPT-3). For encoder-decoder models like T5 or BlenderBot, `max_tokens` should not exceed the model's maximum output length. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`max_new_tokens`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_new_tokens) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.max_total_tokens
documentation: The maximum number of tokens including both the generated result and the input tokens. Only allowed for decoder-only models. Only one argument between `max_tokens` and `max_total_tokens` is allowed. Default value is the model's maximum length. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`max_length`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_length) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.min_tokens
documentation: The minimum number of tokens to generate. Default value is 0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`min_new_tokens`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.min_new_tokens) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.min_total_tokens
documentation: The minimum number of tokens including both the generated result and the input tokens. Only allowed for decoder-only models. Only one argument between `min_tokens` and `min_total_tokens` is allowed. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`min_length`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.min_length) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.n
documentation: The number of independently generated results for the prompt. Not supported when using beam search. Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`num_return_sequences`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.num_return_sequences) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.no_repeat_ngram
documentation: If this exceeds 1, every ngram of that size can only occur once among the generated result (plus the input tokens for decoder-only models). 1 means that this mechanism is disabled (i.e., you cannot prevent 1-gram from being generated repeatedly). Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`no_repeat_ngram_size`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.no_repeat_ngram_size) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.num_beams
documentation: Number of beams for beam search. Numbers between 1 and 31 (both inclusive) are allowed. Default behavior is no beam search. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`num_beams`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.num_beams) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.presence_penalty
documentation: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled at least once in the existing text.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.repetition_penalty
documentation: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared in the generated result (plus the input tokens for decoder-only models). Should be greater than or equal to 1.0 (1.0 means no penalty). See [keskar et al., 2019](https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.05858) for more details. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`repetition_penalty`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.repetition_penalty) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.response_format
documentation:
The enforced format of the model's output.

Note that the content of the output message may be truncated if it exceeds the `max_tokens`.
You can check this by verifying that the `finish_reason` of the output message is `length`.

***Important***
You must explicitly instruct the model to produce the desired output format using a system prompt or user message (e.g., `You are an API generating a valid JSON as output.`).
Otherwise, the model may result in an unending stream of whitespace or other characters.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.seed
documentation: Seed to control random procedure. If nothing is given, the API generate the seed randomly, use it for sampling, and return the seed along with the generated result. When using the `n` argument, you can pass a list of seed values to control all of the independent generations.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.stop
documentation:
When one of the stop phrases appears in the generation result, the API will stop generation.
The stop phrases are excluded from the result.
This option is incompatible with beam search (specified by `num_beams`); use `stop_tokens` for that case instead.
Defaults to empty list.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.stop_tokens
documentation:
Stop generating further tokens when generated token corresponds to any of the tokens in the sequence.
If beam search is enabled, all of the active beams should contain the stop token to terminate generation.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.stream
documentation: Whether to stream generation result. When set true, each token will be sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.temperature
documentation: Sampling temperature. Smaller temperature makes the generation result closer to greedy, argmax (i.e., `top_k = 1`) sampling. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`temperature`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.temperature) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.timeout_microseconds
documentation: Request timeout. Gives the `HTTP 429 Too Many Requests` response status code. Default behavior is no timeout.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.token_index_to_replace
documentation: A list of token indices where to replace the embeddings of input tokens provided via either `tokens` or `prompt`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.top_k
documentation: The number of highest probability tokens to keep for sampling. Numbers between 0 and the vocab size of the model (both inclusive) are allowed. The default value is 0, which means that the API does not apply top-k filtering. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`top_k`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_k) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPromptTypedDict.top_p
documentation: Tokens comprising the top `top_p` probability mass are kept for sampling. Numbers between 0.0 (exclusive) and 1.0 (inclusive) are allowed. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`top_p`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_p) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.prompt
documentation: The prompt (i.e., input text) to generate completions for. Either `prompt` or `tokens` field is required.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.model
documentation: ID of target endpoint. If you want to send request to specific adapter, using \"ENDPOINT_ID:ADAPTER_ROUTE\" format.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.bad_word_tokens
documentation: Same as the above `bad_words` field, but receives token sequences instead of text phrases. This is similar to Hugging Face's [`bad_word_ids`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.bad_words_ids) argument.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.bad_words
documentation:
Text phrases that should not be generated.
For a bad word phrase that contains N tokens, if the first N-1 tokens appears at the last of the generated result, the logit for the last token of the phrase is set to -inf.
Before checking whether a bard word is included in the result, the word is converted into tokens.
We recommend using `bad_word_tokens` because it is clearer.
For example, after tokenization, phrases \"clear\" and \" clear\" can result in different token sequences due to the prepended space character.
Defaults to empty list.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.beam_search_type
documentation: One of `DETERMINISTIC`, `NAIVE_SAMPLING`, and `STOCHASTIC`. Which beam search type to use. `DETERMINISTIC` means the standard, deterministic beam search, which is similar to Hugging Face's [`beam_search`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationMixin.beam_search). Argmuents for controlling random sampling such as `top_k` and `top_p` are not allowed for this option. `NAIVE_SAMPLING` is similar to Hugging Face's [`beam_sample`](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationMixin.beam_sample). `STOCHASTIC` means stochastic beam search (more details in [Kool et al. (2019)](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v97/kool19a.html)). This option is ignored if `num_beams` is not provided. Defaults to `DETERMINISTIC`.

class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.early_stopping
documentation: Whether to stop the beam search when at least `num_beams` beams are finished with the EOS token. Only allowed for beam search. Defaults to false. class_variables.name: friendli.models.dedicatedcompletionsstreambody.DedicatedCompletionsStreamBodyCompletionsBodyWithPrompt.embedding_to_replace

functions.name: friendli._hooks.registration.init_hooks
documentation:
Add hooks by calling hooks.register{sdk_init/before_request/after_success/after_error}Hook
with an instance of a hook that implements that specific Hook interface
Hooks are registered per SDK instance, and are valid for the lifetime of the SDK instance
This file is only ever generated once on the first generation and then is free to be modified.
Any hooks you wish to add should be registered in the init_hooks function. Feel free to define them
in this file or in separate files in the hooks folder.

methods.name: friendli.toolassistedchat.ToolAssistedChat.complete
documentation:
Tool assisted chat completions

Given a list of messages forming a conversation, the model generates a response. Additionally, the model can utilize built-in tools for tool calls, enhancing its capability to provide more comprehensive and actionable responses.

```
param model

Code of the model to use. See [available model list](https://friendli.ai/docs/guides/serverless_endpoints/pricing#text-generation-models).

param messages

A list of messages comprising the conversation so far.

param x_friendli_team

ID of team to run requests as (optional parameter).

param eos_token

A list of endpoint sentence tokens.

param frequency_penalty

Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled, taking into account their frequency in the preceding text. This penalization diminishes the model's tendency to reproduce identical lines verbatim.

param max_tokens

The maximum number of tokens to generate. For decoder-only models like GPT, the length of your input tokens plus  should not exceed the model's maximum length (e.g., 2048 for OpenAI GPT-3). For encoder-decoder models like T5 or BlenderBot,  should not exceed the model's maximum output length. This is similar to Hugging Face's [](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.max_new_tokens) argument.

param min_tokens

The minimum number of tokens to generate. Default value is 0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.generationconfig.min_new_tokens) argument.  This field is unsupported when `tools` are specified.

param n

The number of independently generated results for the prompt. Not supported when using beam search. Defaults to 1. This is similar to Hugging Face's [](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.num_return_sequences) argument.

param parallel_tool_calls

Whether to enable parallel function calling.

param presence_penalty

Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalizes tokens that have been sampled at least once in the existing text.

param repetition_penalty

param response_format

The enforced format of the model's output.  Note that the content of the output message may be truncated if it exceeds the . You can check this by verifying that the  of the output message is .  *Important* You must explicitly instruct the model to produce the desired output format using a system prompt or user message (e.g., ). Otherwise, the model may result in an unending stream of whitespace or other characters.

param resume_generation

Enable to continue text generation even after an error occurs during a tool call.  Note that enabling this option may use more tokens, as the system generates additional content to handle errors gracefully. However, if the system fails more than 8 times, the generation will stop regardless.  *Tip* This is useful in scenarios where you want to maintain text generation flow despite errors, such as when generating long-form content. The user will not be interrupted by tool call issues, ensuring a smoother experience.

param seed

Seed to control random procedure. If nothing is given, random seed is used for sampling, and return the seed along with the generated result. When using the  argument, you can pass a list of seed values to control all of the independent generations.

param stop

When one of the stop phrases appears in the generation result, the API will stop generation. The stop phrases are excluded from the result. Defaults to empty list.

param stream

Whether to stream generation result. When set true, each token will be sent as [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#event_stream_format) once generated.

param temperature

Sampling temperature. Smaller temperature makes the generation result closer to greedy, argmax (i.e., ) sampling. Defaults to 1.0. Request timeout. Gives the  response status code. Default behavior is no timeout.

param tool_choice

Determines the tool calling behavior of the model. When set to , the model will bypass tool execution and generate a response directly. In  mode (the default), the model dynamically decides whether to call a tool or respond with a message. Alternatively, setting  ensures that the model invokes at least one tool before responding to the user. You can also specify a particular tool by .

param top_p

Tokens comprising the top  probability mass are kept for sampling. Numbers between 0.0 (exclusive) and 1.0 (inclusive) are allowed. Defaults to 1.0. This is similar to Hugging Face's [](https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.26.0/en/main_classes/text_generation#transformers.GenerationConfig.top_p) argument.
```
